title: Borehole stress meter system and method for determining wellbore formation instability
abstract: A Wellbore stress meter system and method for determining wellbore formation instability, comprising a first load cell (10), a first pressure sensor (20) with a pressure output signal (20s), a wireless communication system (100), a cable (8), and a surface device (70), said first load cell (10) comprises; - a second pressure sensor (11) with a stress output signal (11s), - a cell element (12) comprising a fluid (12f), - a first interface element (13) in a first end (10a) of said first load cell (10) with fluidly separated first and second surfaces (13a, 13b,) wherein said first surface (13a) is in fluid communication with said fluid (12f), and said first interface element (13) moves relative said cell element (12) as a function of a force (F1) applied on said first surface (13a), and compresses said fluid (12f) acting on said second pressure sensor (11).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A well bore stress meter system (9) comprising a first load cell ( 10), a first pressure sensor (20) with a pressure output signal (20s), a wireless communication system ( 100) comprising an external device ( 110) and an internal device ( 120), a cable (8), and a surface device (70), wherein said first load cell ( 10), said first pressure sensor (20) and said external device ( 110) are configured to be arranged outside said wellbore conduit (2), and said internal device (120) and said cable (8) are configured to be arranged inside said wellbore conduit (2), wherein said first load cell ( 10) comprises; - a second pressure sensor ( 11) with a stress output signal ( l is), - a cell element ( 12) comprising a first fluid ( 12f) with a first fluid pressure ( 12p), - a first interface element ( 13) arranged in a first end ( 10a) of said first load cell ( 10) with fluidly separated first and second surfaces ( 13a, 13b,) wherein said first surface (13a) is in fluid communication with said first fluid ( 12f), and said first interface element (13) is further configured to move relative said cell element ( 12) as a function of a first force (Fl) applied on said first surface ( 13a) relative a second end ( 10b) opposite said first end ( 10a), and to compress said first fluid ( 12f) acting on said second pressure sensor ( 11), wherein said wellbore stress meter system (9) is arranged to transfer said stress output signal ( l is) and said pressure output signal (20s), to said surface device (70) via said wireless communication system ( 100) and said cable (8). 2. A wellbore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims above, wherein said cell element ( 12) is configured for being fixed to said wellbore conduit (2). 3. A wellbore stress meter system (9) according to claim 2, comprising a focal stress receptacle (40) configured for being arranged in said investigation interval and housing said first load cell ( 10), wherein said focal stress receptacle (40) is configured to act on said second surface ( 13b) of said first interface element ( 13) with said first force (Fl) when said focal stress receptacle (40) is subject to a second force (F2) from surrounding masses in said investigation interval. 4. A wellbore stress meter system (9) according to claim 3, wherein said focal stress receptacle (40) is arranged to be cemented in place outside said wellbore conduit (2). 5. A wellbore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims above, wherein said cell element ( 12) is a cylinder and said first interface element ( 13) is a piston arranged to move inside said cylinder ( 12). 6. A wellbore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims 1 to 6 above, 

 wherein said first interface element ( 13) is a bellow or a diaphragm. 7. A well bore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims above wherein said second pressure sensor ( 11) is a quartz pressure sensor. 8. A well bore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims above wherein said first pressure sensor (20) is a quartz pressure and temperature transducer. 9. A well bore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims above, wherein said first load cell ( 10) comprises; - a second interface element ( 130) arranged in said second end ( 10b) of said first load cell ( 10) with fluidly separated first and second surfaces (130a, 130b,) wherein said first surface ( 130a) is in fluid communication with said first fluid ( 12f), and said second interface element ( 130) is further configured to move relative said cell element ( 12) as a function of said first force (Fl). 10. A well bore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims above, comprising; - a pressure sensor housing (80), comprising said first pressure sensor (20) with said output pressure signal (20s), - a first oil filled chamber (81), - a pressure transfer means (82) between said first oil filled chamber (81) and said first pressure sensor (20), arranged to isolate said first pressure sensor (20) from said oil filled chamber (81), and - a pressure permeable filter port (83) through a wall of said housing (80), wherein said pressure permeable filter port (83) is in hydrostatic connectivity with said first oil filled chamber (81). 11 A well bore stress meter system (9) according to claim 10, wherein said housing (5) is integrated with said first load cell ( 10). 12. A well bore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims above, comprising; - a signal processing unit (30) configured for being arranged in said investigation interval. - receiving said stress output signal ( l is) and pressure output signal (20s), - sending said stress output signal ( l is) and pressure output signal (20s) to a communication port (38) on said signal processing unit (30). A well bore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims above, 

 wherein said a surface processing device (70) is configured for; - recording first values (30) for said stress output signal (l is) and said pressure output signal (20s), - periodically reading next values (31) for said stress output signal ( l is) and said pressure output signal (20s), and - detecting a well bore formation instability based on a difference between said next values (31) and said first values (30). 14. A well bore stress meter system (9) according to claim 12 or 13, wherein said signal processing unit (30) is arranged for modulating said stress output signal ( l is) and said pressure output signal (20s) onto a common carrier signal. 15. A well bore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims above, wherein said wireless link is configured to transfer power from said surface device (70) to said internal device ( 120), said internal device ( 120) configured for generating a varying electromagnetic field from said power, and said external device ( 110) is configured to provide power to said signal processing unit (30) by power harvesting said varying electromagnetic field. 16. A well bore stress meter system (9) according to any of the claims above, comprising a second load cell (200) arranged perpendicular to said first load cell ( 10), arranged to detect a third force (F3) acting on said second load cell (200), wherein said third force (F3) is perpendicular to said first force (Fl). 17. A well bore stress meter system (9) according to the claims above comprising a second load cell (200) arranged perpendicular to said first load cell ( 10), arranged to detect a third force (F3) acting on said second load cell (200), wherein said third force (F3) is perpendicular to said first force (Fl), wherein a second stress output signal (211s) from said second load cell (200) is connected to said signal processing unit (30), and said signal processing unit (30) is configured for receiving said second stress output signal (211s) and sending said second stress output signal (211s) to said communication port (38) on said signal processing unit (30). 18. A method for determining a well bore formation instability, comprising the steps of; - arranging a first pressure sensor (20) with a pressure output signal (20s) and a first load cell ( 10) in an investigation interval outside a well bore conduit, wherein said first load cell ( 10) comprises; - a second pressure sensor ( 11) with a stress output signal ( l is), 

 - a cell element ( 12) comprising a first fluid ( 12f) with a first fluid pressure ( 12p), - a first interface element ( 13) arranged in a first end ( 10a) of said first load cell ( 10) with fluidly separated first and second surfaces ( 13a, 13b,) wherein said first surface ( 13a) is in fluid communication with said first fluid ( 12f), and said first interface element (13) is further configured to move relative said cell element ( 12) as a function of a first force (Fl) applied on said first surface ( 13a) relative a second end ( 10b) opposite said first end ( 10a), and to compress said first fluid ( 12f) acting on said second pressure sensor ( 11), - transmitting wirelessly said stress output signal ( l is) and said pressure output signal (20s) across a wall of said well bore conduit and further via cable (8) inside said well bore conduit (2) to a surface device (70), wherein said method comprises the steps of in said surface device (70); - recording first values (30) for said stress output signal (l is) and said pressure output signal (20s), - periodically reading next values (31) for said stress output signal ( l is) and said pressure output signal (20s), and - detecting a well bore formation instability based on a difference between said next values (31) and said first values (30). 19. A method for determining a well bore formation instability according to claim 18, comprising the step of providing power from said surface device (70) via cable (8) downhole inside said well bore conduit (2) and further via wireless transmission through said wall of said well bore conduit to said investigation interval. 20. A method for determining a well bore formation instability according to claim 18 or 19, wherein said first pressure sensor (20) is a quartz pressure and temperature transducer, comprising the step of correcting said stress output signal (l is) based on a p re-determined relation between a temperature measured by temperature sensor arranged in said investigation interval and said stress output signal ( l is). 21. A method for determining a well bore formation instability according to claim 20, comprising the step of initially determining said relation between said temperature measured by temperature sensor arranged in said investigation interval and said stress output signal ( l is) by arranging said load cell inside a block of cement, varying said temperature, and recording a variation in said stress output signal ( l is) as a function of said temperature variation.
